mnemonics.title: Divide Conquer Combine Steps
mnemonics.content: DCC: Don't Create Chaos - Divide problem, Conquer subproblems, Combine solutions

mnemonics.title: Master Theorem Cases
mnemonics.content: If f(n) is poly less, answer is n^logb(a); If equal, multiply by log n; If poly more, f(n) wins

acronyms.usage: Algorithm design paradigm breaking problems into subproblems

acronyms.expansion: Time complexity function of input size n
acronyms.usage: Used to express recurrence relations for algorithm analysis
Formula to solve recurrence relations of form T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n)

acronyms.expansion: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's uses D&C concept)
acronyms.usage: Finding contiguous subarray with maximum sum

visualTips.visualization: Draw a binary tree with n elements at leaves, merging upward. At each level, total work is n, and depth is log n, giving n log n total.
Imagine pivot as a 'king' - all smaller elements go to left court, larger to right court. The king finds its final position.
Think of searching in a phone book - open middle, decide which half, repeat. Visualize cutting array in half each step.
Compare f(n) with n^logb(a) on a log-log plot. Which grows faster determines the solution.

analogies.analogy: Like conquering a kingdom by dividing army into smaller groups to capture different regions, then combining forces.
Sort books by dividing into two piles, sorting each pile, then merging them back in order.
Finding a word in dictionary by opening middle, deciding which half, repeating - like cutting search space in half.

analogies.concept: Quick Sort in partitioning books by genre
analogies.analogy: Pick a genre (pivot), put all that genre together, then sort each genre group separately.
